title: Tank Damage Simulator Beta Browser
subtitle: Curated local replay pack for the current AP and HE prototype scenarios.

tanks.name: Showcase Tank A
tanks.description: Primary showcase medium tank with a readable driver hatch weak spot, sloped glacis, and a turret layout that supports crew-focused AP cases.

tanks.name: Showcase Tank B
tanks.description: Contrast assault gun with a tougher frontal package than Tank A and a weaker right side that exposes fuel and reserve ammunition.

shells.name: 75 mm AP Shot (Prototype)
shells.description: Baseline AP round for frontal A vs B comparison and most side-penetration checks.

shells.name: 88 mm AP Shot (Prototype)
shells.description: Stronger AP round for tougher front plates, roof entries, and turret crew knockout cases.

shells.name: 75 mm HE Shell (Prototype)
shells.description: Lighter HE shell for hatch detonation and fuse-failure contrast cases.

shells.name: 105 mm HE Shell (Prototype)
shells.description: Heavier HE shell for the short-range right-side blast showcase case.

categories.label: AP penetration
categories.description: Clean AP defeats against weaker plates and side or rear exposures.

categories.label: AP ricochet / no penetration
categories.description: Frontal AP cases where armor slope, thickness, or range holds.

categories.label: HE detonation
categories.description: Armed HE impacts that produce a visible internal damage story.

categories.label: HE fuse failure
categories.description: Glancing or resistant impacts where HE stops short of arming.

categories.label: Module damage
categories.description: Cases where the shot is meant to cut through systems rather than center on crew.

categories.label: Crew knockout
categories.description: Shots that are curated to incapacitate crew without turning into a generic ammo-rack demo.

categories.label: Comparison cases
categories.description: Best side-by-side showcases for the same shell family on different targets.

categories.label: Vulnerability paths
categories.description: Curated roof, rear, and weak-spot shots that show where the current beta sandbox is easiest to read.

categories.label: Range contrast
categories.description: Same family, same target, different engagement range with a materially different result.

scenarios.name: Tank A front plate penetration
scenarios.description: Baseline AP front hit on Tank A that should pierce the frontal plate and continue into front-drive internals.
scenarios.comparisonLabel: Tank A vs Tank B frontal AP baseline

scenarios.name: Tank A glacis ricochet
scenarios.description: Shallow AP impact on Tank A's glacis that demonstrates slope-driven deflection and surface scarring.

scenarios.name: Tank A right-side module damage
scenarios.description: Close AP side shot that is curated to chain through the fuel tank and engine rather than focus on crew.

scenarios.name: Tank A rear engine damage
scenarios.description: Rear AP hit on Tank A's thin back plate that should drive fragments through the engine bay rather than the fighting compartment.

scenarios.name: Tank A turret crew knockout
scenarios.description: Stronger AP turret hit that is tuned to incapacitate the gunner without centering on the ammo rack.

scenarios.name: Tank A driver hatch detonation
scenarios.description: Lighter HE hit on the vulnerable hatch area that arms the fuse and damages both crew and transmission.

scenarios.name: Tank A glacis fuse failure
scenarios.description: HE glancing front hit on the stronger glacis where projected resistance stays below the fuse threshold.

scenarios.name: Tank A frontal long-range no-penetration
scenarios.description: Long-range version of the Tank A frontal baseline where the same 75 mm AP family falls off enough to stop on the upper glacis.
scenarios.comparisonLabel: Tank A short vs long-range 75 mm AP

scenarios.name: Tank B frontal no-penetration
scenarios.description: Frontal AP comparison case showing the same shell family fail against Tank B's stronger upper glacis.
scenarios.comparisonLabel: Tank A vs Tank B frontal AP baseline

scenarios.name: Tank B roof crew knockout
scenarios.description: Top-down AP entry through Tank B's thin casemate roof that should disable crew in the compact fighting compartment.

scenarios.name: Tank B right-side HE blast
scenarios.description: Heavy HE side-blast showcase on Tank B that detonates and destroys the exposed right-side ammunition reserve.
